title: Order relative to authorizing the joint committee on Financial Services to make an investigation and study of certain current Senate documents relative to financial services matters.

abstracts.abstract: Senate, March 5, 2026 -- The committee on Financial Services, to whom was referred the petitions (accompanied by bill, Senate, No. 676) of Michael D. Brady for legislation relative to dental insurance plans; (accompanied by bill, Senate, No. 700) of Brendan P. Crighton for legislation requiring insurance companies to notify policyholders of the use of drones; (accompanied by bill, Senate, No. 736) of James B. Eldridge for legislation to establish a Massachusetts public bank; and (accompanied by bill, Senate, No. 767) of Patricia D. Jehlen for legislation relative to the divestment from entity which manufactures, produces, promotes, sells, or distributes holds weapons of mass destruction, report the accompanying Order  (Senate, No. 2989).

actions.description: Reported from the committee on Financial Services
Accompanied by S676, S700, S736 and S767
Order reported favorably by committee and referred to the committee on Rules of the two branches, acting concurrently

related_entities.name: committee on Rules of the two branches, acting concurrently
the committee on Rules of the two branches, acting concurrently

actions.description: Discharged to the committee on Senate Rules
